system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

startFrom       startTime;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - Inlet straight section (x: -50 to 0, y: -25 to 25)
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 0
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 1
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 2
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 3
    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 4
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 5
    (   0  25  25 )   // 6
    ( -50  25  25 )   // 7

    // Block 1 - Upper bend arm (x: 0 to 25, y: -25 to 0)
    (  25 -25  25 )   // 8
    (  25   0  25 )   // 9
    (   0   0  25 )   // 10
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 11

    // Block 2 - Outlet transition (x: 25 to -100, y: -75 to -25)
    (  25 -75 -25 )   // 12
    ( -100 -75 -25 )  // 13
    ( -100 -25 -25 )  // 14
    (  25 -25 -25 )   // 15
    (  25 -75  25 )   // 16
    ( -100 -75  25 )  // 17
    ( -100 -25  25 )  // 18
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    // Vertices 0,1,2,3 at x=-50; 4,5,6,7 at x=0
    // Back face (x=-50): 0,1,2,3 = CCW when viewed from -x
    // Front face (x=0): 4,5,6,7 = CCW when viewed from +x
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Upper bend arm
    // Back face (x=0): 4,5,6,7 = CCW when viewed from -x
    // Front face (x=25): 8,9,10,11 = CCW when viewed from +x
    // Shared with Block 0: face 4,5,6,7
    hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet transition
    // Back face (x=25): 8,9,10,11 = CCW when viewed from -x
    // Front face (x=-100): 12,13,14,15 = need CCW from +x
    // Current: 12(25,-75,-25), 13(-100,-75,-25), 14(-100,-25,-25), 15(25,-25,-25)
    // From +x: 12(right,bottom), 13(left,bottom), 14(left,top), 15(right,top) = CW ✗
    // Need: 12->15->14->13 = CCW
    hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4);  // x = -50 face
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (12 13 14 15);  // x = -100 face
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (

            (7 6 2 3);  // z = 25 face
            (4 8 9 5);  // y = 25 face
            (7 3 1 2);  // x = -50 face (shared with interior)
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 5 6 2);  // z = 25 face
            (0 4 8 1);  // z = -25 face
            (12 13 14 15); // y = -75 face
        );
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 20 4); // z = -25 face
            (4 20 21 5); // y = 0 face
        );
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (7 2 22 6); // z = 25 face
            (7 22 23 6); // y = 0 face
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
    (0 1 2 3) (4 5 6 7)
    (4 5 6 7) (8 9 10 11)
    (8 9 10 11) (12 13 14 15)
);

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) ... is inside-out` — Block 1 has inward-pointing faces.
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find "points" — cascading from blockMesh failure.

## Root Cause Across All 9 Attempts

Every attempt has tried to fix vertex ordering within the existing block topology. The fundamental problem is that the **block topology itself creates non-convex or degenerate hexahedra** where shared faces between blocks don't form proper planar quadrilaterals. The bend geometry creates complex multi-way junctions where the blocks intersect at non-planar or trapezoidal faces, making it impossible to satisfy OpenFOAM's convex-hexahedron requirement through vertex ordering alone.

## New Approach: Use Exactly 3 Blocks with Clean, Convex Geometry

The key insight from the previous failures is that the block connectivity diagram needs to be redesigned so that **every pair of adjacent blocks shares a full, planar quadrilateral face**. Here is the correct 3-block topology:

### Block 0: Inlet Straight Section (x = -50 to x = 0, y = -25 to y = 25)
```
hex (3 2 1 0 7 6 5 4)
```
- Back face (0,1,2,3) at x=-50: CCW when viewed from -x ✓
- Front face (4,5,6,7) at x=0: CCW when viewed from +x ✓

### Block 1: Upper Bend Arm (x = 0 to x = 25, y = -25 to y = 0)
```
hex (7 11 10 6 3 15 14 2)
```
- Face at x=0: vertices (7,6,10,11) — verify CCW from -x
- Face at x=25: vertices (2,14,15,3) — verify CCW from +x
- Shared face with Block 0: vertices (2,3,7,6). Block 0 with ordering `(3 2 1 0 7 6 5 4)` has this face as its "top" face. Block 1 must wind it as **(2 3 15 14)** to be opposite. The full block definition should be `hex (7 11 10 6 2 3 15 14)`.

### Block 2: Combined Lower Bend & Outlet Transition (x = 25 to x = -100, y = -75 to y = -25)
```
hex (12 15 14 13 16 17 11 10)
```
- Face at x=25: vertices (12,13,14,15) — verify CCW from -x
- Face at x=-100: vertices (10,11,17,16) — verify CCW from +x
- Shared face with Block 1: vertices (10,11,14,15). Block 1's far face is (2,14,15,3). These don't match — this reveals the geometry needs adjustment.

## The Real Fix: Match the Tutorial's Proven Block Topology

The user requirement says "Follow the same geometry as the tutorial." The **squareBend tutorial from OpenFOAM.org** uses a specific, validated block topology. The current custom blockMeshDict has been iteratively broken across 9 attempts. 

**Copy the EXACT blockMeshDict from the official squareBend tutorial** (compressible/rhoSimpleFoam), then **only replace the vertex coordinate values** with the user's specified positions. The tutorial's block topology is mathematically proven to produce valid convex hexahedra with consistent shared faces.

Find the tutorial's `blockMeshDict` online. It typically has ~3-4 blocks with vertex counts like `(10 10 10)`. The vertex ordering and block connectivity in the tutorial are correct. Simply map the tutorial's vertex coordinates to:

- Replace tutorial vertex 0 with (-50, 25, 25), vertex 1 with (-50, 25, -25), etc., following the user's specification exactly
- Keep all block definitions, edge definitions, boundary definitions, and mergePatchPairs identical to the tutorial

Then update the boundary condition files (`0/U`, `0/p`, `0/T`, `0/k`, `0/nut`, `0/alphat`, `0/epsilon`) so their patch names match whatever the tutorial uses (likely `inlet`, `outlet`, `walls` — check the tutorial's `0/` files).

## Error 2: Cascading `rhoSimpleFoam` error

This will resolve automatically once `blockMesh` succeeds.</review_analysis>

Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000.
